rag_rules: Validator diagnostics pointing to unreachable or invalid port specs indicate incorrect feature references in implementation contracts; resolve by ensuring all port references are valid and visible in the current scope.
When diagnostics report 'not of an adequate kind', verify that port types match expected AADL kind (e.g., data port vs. event port) in the implementation scope.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, always reference only visible features or declared helpers from the same annex; avoid introducing new architecture elements to fix type errors.
Assign statements in implementation scope must target existing output features or declared auxiliary variables; guarantees should not be placed in implementation scope.
For minimal repair, trace unresolved identifiers in guarantee expressions and fix root causes rather than adding new architecture elements.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must use only visible identifiers or declared helpers within the same annex; do not reference undefined or inaccessible features.
Implementation-level contracts should use assign for output bindings and avoid guarantee statements in implementation scope.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to resolve port type mismatches by introducing new component types or connections; instead, correct the feature references in the existing scope.
Avoid adding new architecture elements to fix AGREE diagnostics; instead, trace and correct the root cause of unresolved identifiers or invalid expressions.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about entity references not of adequate kind
Component type vs. implementation scope placement rules
